title: 7 High-Dividend Stocks to Navigate 4 Growing Market Risks
author: David Wagner
description: Markets face headwinds from rising inflation (3.8% YoY), elevated bond yields (10-year Treasury at 4.631%), ongoing Iran conflict, and upcoming NVIDIA earnings. The article recommends high-dividend stocks with 5%+ yields and 20%+ upside potential as defensive positions, highlighting Columbia Banking System and T. Rowe Price Group as examples of financially resilient companies with strong dividend histories.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest regional bank in northwestern US with strong dividend profile (5.1% yield), solid financial strength (CET1 ratio above requirements), improving net interest margin (3.84%), and $700M buyback program. Trading at attractive 9.55x forward earnings.

Major independent asset manager with $1.7 trillion AUM, 40-year consecutive dividend increase history, current yield above 5.4%, and strong cash generation ($629M returned to shareholders in Q1 2026) despite cautious investment environment.

Upcoming earnings report on Wednesday identified as important test for AI-related stocks. Weak results or cautious guidance could trigger profit-taking across tech sector, though company has been major market rally contributor.

title: Columbia Banking System Announces $0.37 Per Common Share Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Columbia Banking System, Inc. (NASDAQ:COLB) announced that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.37 per common share, payable on June 15, 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 29, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Dividend announcements are typically viewed positively as they indicate stable earnings and management confidence in future cash flows.

title: Columbia Banking System Announces $0.37 Per Common Share Dividend

description: Columbia Banking System, Inc. (NASDAQ:COLB) announced that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.37 per common share, payable on March 16, 2026 to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026.

Dividend announcements are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable earnings and management confidence in future performance.

title: Columbia Financial, Inc. Announces Seventh Stock Repurchase Program
author: Columbia Financial, Inc.
description: Columbia Financial, Inc. authorized a new stock repurchase program to acquire up to 1,800,000 shares (approximately 1.7% of outstanding common stock), following approval from the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The stock repurchase program suggests confidence in the company's financial health and potential future growth, as management believes the stock is undervalued or wants to return value to shareholders
Mentioned as a subsidiary of Columbia Financial, with no specific positive or negative indicators in the text
